---
name: obligatory_ai-generated-comment
description: >
  Enforce the AI-generation comment standard on every newly created file and every newly created class.
  Use this skill whenever a new file is created, a new class is defined, or existing code is significantly
  regenerated by AI. This is mandatory for all code generation, refactoring, and update tasks — including
  Java version updates, migrations, or any other AI-assisted code changes.
---

# Skill: AI-Generated Comment Guideline

## Purpose

Ensure every AI-generated file and class is clearly identified with a standard traceability comment so the
codebase remains auditable and transparent about AI involvement.

## Rule (Non-Negotiable)

For **every newly created file** and **every newly created class**, include the following comment text **exactly**:

```
generated by AI in Github cloud
```

## Placement Rules

| Scope | Where to place the comment |
|---|---|
| New file | Near the **top of the file**, before imports or package declarations where the language allows it; otherwise as the very first comment block |
| New class | **In or directly above** the class declaration (e.g., as a Javadoc/block comment, or an inline comment on the line immediately preceding `class`) |

> Keep the text exact — do not paraphrase, translate, or abbreviate. Consistency is required for tooling and auditing to work correctly.

## Language-Specific Examples

### Java
```java
// generated by AI in Github cloud
public class HelloWorld {
    ...
}
```

Or as a Javadoc block:
```java
/**
 * generated by AI in Github cloud
 */
public class HelloWorld {
    ...
}
```

### Python
```python
# generated by AI in Github cloud
class HelloWorld:
    ...
```

### TypeScript / JavaScript
```typescript
// generated by AI in Github cloud
export class HelloWorld {
    ...
}
```

## Checklist Before Committing

- [ ] Every new `.java` / `.py` / `.ts` / `.js` (or other) file has the comment near the top
- [ ] Every new class definition has the comment in or directly above it
- [ ] Comment text matches **exactly**: `generated by AI in Github cloud`
- [ ] Existing files that are only lightly edited (no new class/file) do NOT need the comment added retroactively
